questions: What is pacing in advertising and why does it matter?
How does pacing affect delivery and performance?
qa.answer: Pacing is controlling spend over time to match budget and performance goals, avoiding front‑loading or underdelivery. Good pacing stabilizes learning, frequency, and CPA/ROAS across the flight.
implication: Set daily/weekly pacing targets and allowable deviation bands.
Use bid caps and budget adjustments to correct over/under‑pacing early.
Monitor hourly/day‑part pacing where applicable to align with demand.

questions: How do you operationalize pacing in platforms?
qa.answer: Levers include campaign budget types, bid strategies, delivery modes, frequency caps, and inventory/geo scheduling. Forecasts and guardrails guide reallocation without disrupting learning.
implication: Choose delivery modes that respect pacing (standard vs. accelerated).
Set frequency caps and day‑parting to match audience availability.
Institute weekly reviews to correct drift and prevent end‑of‑flight spikes.
